# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A – Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question 1
**[KTU University Exam – Model Paper 2024]**
*List the three performance criteria a fire door must satisfy as per IS 3614 (Part 2). Briefly explain any one.*

**Model Answer:**

A fire door must satisfy three performance criteria, often called the **"ESI" criteria**:

1. **Integrity (E):** The door must prevent the passage of flames and hot gases through its body or gaps. This is verified using the **cotton pad test** — a cotton wool pad held 20–30 mm from the un-exposed face must not ignite due to escaping gases.

2. **Stability (S):** The door assembly must remain in place and self-supporting for the entire test duration without collapse or detachment.

3. **Insulation (I):** The mean temperature rise on the un-exposed face must not exceed **140 °C** above ambient, and no single point should exceed **180 °C** above ambient. This prevents fire spread by conduction.

---

### Question 2
**[KTU University Exam – Model Paper 2024]**
*What is the role of intumescent seals in fire doors? Mention the expansion ratio typically observed.*

**Model Answer:**

**Intumescent seals** are heat-reactive strips (usually graphite-based or sodium silicate-based) installed in grooves on the door leaf edges, frame, or both.

**Role:**
- At normal temperatures, they remain inert and compact.
- When exposed to fire (above ~200 °C), they **expand 10 to 30 times** their original volume, sealing the gaps between the door leaf and the frame.
- This prevents the passage of **flames, hot gases, and smoke**, maintaining the **integrity (E)** criterion.

**Expansion Ratio:** Typically **10× to 30×** (volume), reaching full expansion in 5–10 minutes of fire exposure.

---

## 5.2 Part B – ESE Module Internal Choice (14 Marks Each)

### Question A (14 Marks) – **[KTU University Exam – Dec 2024 Style]**

**Question A (a)** — *7 Marks*

Explain in detail the construction, materials, and BIS specification requirements for **Plate Type Fire Doors** as per **IS 3614 (Part 1): 2021**.

**Model Solution:**

A plate type (flush) fire door consists of a sandwich panel construction comprising an inner core of fire-resistant material bonded between two facings.

**1. Construction:**

- **Door Leaf:** 44 mm to 55 mm thick, flush-faced, rectangular.
- **Core Materials:** Calcium silicate board, vermiculite board, mineral wool, perlite, or a combination. Core density typically 400–900 kg/m³.
- **Facings/Skins:** 
  - Steel-faced: 0.8–1.5 mm galvanized steel sheets
  - Timber-faced: 6 mm commercial ply or hardwood (teak)
- **Stiles and Rails:** Hardwood (teak/sal) or steel — minimum 75 mm width for hinge support.
- **Edge Seals:** Intumescent strips (graphite-based, 2 mm × 10 mm) in grooves along edges.

**2. BIS Specifications (IS 3614 Part 1):**
- Leaf thickness tolerance: **±2 mm**
- Squareness tolerance: **±3 mm** per 1000 mm of leaf height
- Hardware: 3 heavy-duty ball-bearing hinges (minimum 100 mm × 75 mm × 3 mm)
- Self-closing device: mandatory, must close door from any angle
- Fire-rated lockset: must function after fire exposure test
- Vision panel (if provided): fire-rated glass with intumescent interlayer, max **0.5 m²** area

**3. Performance Requirements (IS 3614 Part 2):**
- Must satisfy ESI criteria for 60, 90, or 120 minutes.
- Cotton pad test, hose stream test (where applicable).
- Cycle test: 50,000 opening/closing cycles without failure.

**4. Marking:** Each door shall be permanently marked (riveted metal plate) with:
- Manufacturer's name
- BIS certification mark
- Fire rating (FD 60 / FD 90 / FD 120)
- IS standard reference

**5. Application:** Offices, hospitals, hotels, residential stairwells, IT server rooms.

---

**Question A (b)** — *7 Marks*

Describe the testing procedure for fire doors as per **IS 3614 (Part 2)**. Include the standard fire curve equation and explain the insulation criterion.

**Model Solution:**

**1. Test Specimen Preparation:**
- A full-size door assembly (including frame, hardware, seals) is installed in a test wall.
- Specimen is conditioned at standard laboratory conditions (23 ± 2 °C, 50 ± 5% RH) for **at least 28 days** before testing.

**2. Furnace and Instrumentation:**
- The specimen is mounted on the furnace, with the fire side exposed.
- **At least 5 disc thermocouples** are fixed on the unexposed face.
- Furnace thermocouples are placed as per IS 3614 Pt 2 / BS 476 Pt 20.
- Pressure differential: **8–20 Pa** across the specimen (positive pressure on un-exposed side).

**3. Standard Fire Curve (Heating):**

$$
T(t) = T_0 + 345 \log_{10}(8t + 1)
$$

Where $T$ = furnace temperature (°C), $T_0$ = initial temperature (°C), $t$ = time (min).

**Sample Calculations:**

| $t$ (min) | $T$ (°C) at $T_0$ = 20 °C |
|:---:|:---:|
| 0 | 20 |
| 30 | 869 |
| 60 | 968 |
| 120 | 1066 |

**4. Criteria Evaluated During Test:**

(a) **Integrity (E):**
- Cotton pad test — no ignition for 30 s when held 20–30 mm from cracks.
- No sustained flaming on un-exposed face for ≥ 10 s.

(b) **Stability (S):**
- Door must not collapse, deflect excessively, or detach from frame.
- Maximum deflection: typically ≤ height/100 (specified by BIS).

(c) **Insulation (I):**
- **Mean temperature rise** on un-exposed face ≤ **140 °C** above ambient.
- **Maximum single-point rise** ≤ **180 °C** above ambient.

**5. Hose Stream Test (Optional):**
- For certain ratings, post-heating water spray simulates firefighter impact.
- Specimen must retain integrity and insulation after cooling.

**6. Rating Determination:**
- The fire resistance rating is the **minimum time** at which any of E, S, or I is breached.
- Rating is recorded as: **FD 60, FD 90, FD 120, or FD 240**.

---

### Question B (14 Marks) – **[KTU University Exam – Dec 2024 Style]**

**Question B (a)** — *7 Marks*

Explain the construction and BIS specification requirements for **Metal Covered Fire Doors** as per **IS 3614 (Part 1)**. Compare briefly with Plate Type doors.

**Model Solution:**

**1. Construction of Metal Covered Fire Doors:**

A metal covered fire door (also called hollow metal door) consists of two formed steel sheets welded or interlocked together to create a hollow shell, then **filled with a fire-resistant insulating core**.

**Key Construction Elements:**

| Component | Specification |
|-----------|---------------|
| **Outer Skin** | 0.8–1.5 mm CRCA/GI steel sheet |
| **Inner Skin** | 0.8–1.5 mm CRCA/GI steel sheet |
| **Core** | Mineral wool (48–96 kg/m³ density), ceramic fibre, or calcium silicate |
| **Core Thickness** | 50–70 mm |
| **Frame** | 1.2–2.0 mm press-formed steel, hot-dip galvanized (IS 4759) |
| **Hinges** | Heavy-duty, stainless steel, 3 nos. minimum |
| **Closer** | Hydraulically controlled, fire-rated |
| **Locks** | Mortise-type fire-rated lockset |
| **Intumescent Seals** | Required on all edges |

**2. BIS Specifications (IS 3614 Part 1):**
- All joints to be **continuously welded** and ground flush.
- Galvanizing: **610 g/m² minimum** as per **IS 4759** (for severe environments).
- No visible welds on exposed surfaces.
- Hardware fixing: Self-tapping screws or welding with reinforcement plates.
- Acoustic insulation: up to **35–45 dB** (typical).

**3. Comparison with Plate Type Doors:**

| Parameter | Plate Type | Metal Covered |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| Core | Calcium silicate / vermiculite | Mineral wool / ceramic fibre |
| Facings | GI steel OR timber ply | GI steel (both sides) |
| Typical Rating | FD 60–120 | FD 120–240 |
| Strength | Moderate | Very high (impact-resistant) |
| Application | Commercial / residential | Industrial / high-hazard |
| Cost | Lower | Higher (but more durable) |

**4. Application:** Transformer rooms, substations, factories, warehouses, basements, power plants, high-security zones.

---

**Question B (b)** — *7 Marks*

Describe the construction, working, and BIS specification of **Rolling Type Fire Doors / Fire Shutters** as per **IS 3808 / IS 14609**.

**Model Solution:**

**1. Definition:**
A rolling fire door (rolling shutter) is a curtain of interlocking galvanized steel slats wound around a barrel at the top of the opening. It descends vertically along guide channels on either side of the opening during a fire event, providing fire compartmentalization.

**2. Components:**

| Component | Material & Specification |
|-----------|--------------------------|
| **Slats** | 75 mm wide × 1.0–1.25 mm thick GI steel, interlocking profile |
| **Barrel/Pipe** | MS pipe 100–150 mm diameter, 3.2 mm thick |
| **Guide Channels** | 50–75 mm wide × 1.6 mm thick GI steel |
| **Bottom Bar** | T-section or L-section, 50 mm × 25 mm × 3 mm |
| **Brackets** | MS plate 6 mm thick, capable of supporting full shutter weight |
| **Drive Mechanism** | Manual chain OR gear motor (3-phase 415 V, IP-55 enclosure) |
| **Governor** | Controls descent speed ≤ **0.2 m/s** (fail-safe gravity drop) |
| **Hood/Cover** | GI sheet 0.8 mm thick (optional) |

**3. Working Principle:**
- **Normal Operation:** Shutter is rolled up and held by an electromagnet/manual brake.
- **Fire Event:** 
  - Fire alarm signal → electromagnet releases → shutter descends by gravity.
  - Governor limits descent to safe speed.
  - Bottom bar reaches floor, sealing the opening.
- **Fail-Safe:** Operates even during complete power failure (gravity-based).

**4. BIS Specifications (IS 3808 / IS 14609):**
- Slats: Galvanized as per **IS 2629** / **IS 2633** (zinc coating 610 g/m²).
- Bottom lock: Push-pull or slide bolt type.
- Gear motor: 1–3 HP, with limit switches (top & bottom positions).
- Slat interlocking: Must withstand **15 kg lateral load** without dislodgement.
- Fire rating: **FD 60 to FD 120** (with proper sealing of slats using intumescent strips).

**5. Testing:**
- Standard fire exposure test as per **IS 3614 Pt 2**.
- Slats must maintain integrity (no flame passage through interlock gaps).
- Guides must remain attached; no collapse.

**6. Application:**
- Factories, warehouses, atria, transformer yards, aircraft hangars, subway/metro stations, large commercial complexes.

---

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Common Pitfalls:**
> 1. **Confusing IS standards** — Many students write "IS 3614" without specifying Part 1 (Specification) or Part 2 (Performance/Test). Always mention both parts!
> 2. **Skipping BIS standard numbers** — Every material/component should reference its BIS code (e.g., IS 4759 for galvanizing, IS 2629 for zinc coating).
> 3. **Ignoring insulation vs. integrity** — These are TWO distinct criteria. Many students treat them as one.
> 4. **Forgetting the cotton pad test** — It is the **most frequently asked** integrity verification method.
> 5. **Not labelling diagram parts** — In construction questions, un-labelled diagrams lose 2–3 marks.
> 6. **Confusing FD rating with FR rating** — FD = Fire Door (in minutes); FR = Fire Resistance (E+I combined).
> 7. **Not mentioning self-closing device** — A fire door without an approved self-closer is **NOT** a fire door (per IS 3614).

---

## 5.3 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

✅ **Quick Revision Checklist:**

- 🔥 **Three Door Types:** Plate (flush), Metal Covered, Rolling (Roller)
- 📋 **Governing Standards:** **IS 3614 (Pt 1 & Pt 2)**, **IS 3808**, **IS 14609**
- 🎯 **Three Criteria (ESI):** **E** (Integrity), **S** (Stability), **I** (Insulation)
- 🌡️ **Insulation Limits:** Mean ΔT ≤ **140 °C**, Single point ΔT ≤ **180 °C**
- 📈 **Standard Fire Curve:** $T = T_0 + 345 \log_{10}(8t + 1)$
- 🧪 **Cotton Pad Test:** No ignition in 30 seconds (Integrity)
- 🚪 **Self-Closing Device:** Mandatory on all fire doors (per IS 3614)
- 🔩 **Intumescent Seals:** Expand **10–30×** on heating; seal gaps
- 🏭 **Plate Type:** 44–55 mm leaf, calcium silicate core, FD 60–120
- 🏗️ **Metal Covered:** 0.8–1.5 mm steel shell, mineral wool core, FD 120–240
- 🎢 **Rolling Type:** 75 mm slats, gravity drop, FD 60–120, governed descent ≤ 0.2 m/s
- 🔧 **Galvanizing:** **IS 4759 / IS 2629 / IS 2633** for corrosion protection
- ⏱️ **Cycle Test:** 50,000 cycles (plate/metal covered); 5,000 cycles (rolling)
- 🧱 **Marking:** Permanent metal plate with manufacturer, BIS mark, FD rating, IS ref
- 🏛️ **NBC 2016 Reference:** Part 4 (Fire and Life Safety) mandates fire doors in stairwells, lift lobbies, service shafts
- 🔁 **Fail-Safe:** Rolling shutters MUST operate on power failure (gravity drop)
